hallucination.rationale: The note fabricates vital signs (HR: 94, Temp: 94, RR: 20, SpO2: 98%) that are not in the transcript. It also lists 'Blood pressure in the office: 140/90' which is not an accurate representation of the transcript data.

instruction_following.rationale: The note retains template bracketed instructions as placeholder text instead of filling them in with actual content (e.g., '[Patient's primary reason for visit]', '[Patient's symptoms and medical history...]'). It fails to follow virtually all template instructions and produces a catastrophically broken output.

completeness.rationale: The note captures almost none of the transcript content. Missing: chief complaint details, HPI narrative, patient's asymptomatic status, family history of stroke, medication adherence issues, counseling on medication timing, lisinopril side effect discussion, diet/sodium counseling, exercise details, alcohol use, follow-up plan details.
missed_items: Chief complaint: blood pressure follow-up prompted by portal message
Family history: father had stroke at age 62
Medication adherence issues (missing 1-2 doses/week, inconsistent timing)
Counseling on anchoring medication to toothbrush routine
Addition of lisinopril 10mg with ACE inhibitor explanation
Exercise: walking dog 20 min, encouraged 30-45 min

template_adherence.rationale: While section headers are present, the content is either placeholder template text or a massively repeated loop of blood pressure entries. The note is essentially non-functional as a clinical document.

entity_marking.rationale: No entity marking was attempted anywhere in the note. No {{drug:...}} or {{condition:...}} syntax is used.

duplication.rationale: The Plan section contains a catastrophic repetition loop of 'Blood pressure in the home: 140/90' and 'Blood pressure in the home: 150/90' repeated hundreds of times.
